mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings expectations upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above a threshold of 5.5% signals weakening labor markets, reducing consumer spending and earnings forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical negative correlation between high unemployment and equity returns
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of cases or average return is greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low jointly indicate strong interest-rate-sensitive demand, boosting construction, consumption, and financial conditions, which lifts S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: early expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (HOUST z-score * negative of FEDFUNDS z-score) versus forward 60-day SPX returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a tight monetary regime (FEDFUNDS above 5%), rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals overheating that prompts further tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 5%, the correlation between INDPRO growth and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) above a level of 240 erodes real purchasing power and raises the risk of monetary tightening, causing immediate negative repricing of equities over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation historically leads to lower equity valuations
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, the median 5-day forward SPX return is greater than 0% or the frequency of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflect a synchronized expansion in labor income and consumption, reinforcing each other to drive strong corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and RSAFS grow month-over-month, the subsequent 20-day SPX return average is not above the unconditional average by at least 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, which supports broader economic output and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year correlation between INDPRO three-month change and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is non-positive or below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound of interest rates (FEDFUNDS below 0.5%), monetary policy is constrained, so retail sales (RSAFS) growth becomes a more powerful signal of demand-driven economic acceleration, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During ZLB periods, the IC between one-month RSAFS growth and 20-day forward SPX returns is below 0.03 or not statistically significant at 10% level.
